summary: Frontend-only validation requested for Butler AI after a heavy refactor (35 native pkgs pruned, 10 files migrated from expo-file-system root → expo-file-system/legacy, 47MB→3.2MB assets stripped, ProGuard disabled, new eas.json). BLOCKER: the Expo web preview at EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL now returns HTTP 500 for /index.bundle?platform=web — Metro fatal: 'Missing "./build/resolveAssetSource" specifier in "expo-asset" package'. The chain is expo-image (used by components/RobotThemeUI.tsx ← imported by app/onboarding_v2.tsx) → react-native/Libraries/Image/resolveAssetSource → expo-asset/./build/resolveAssetSource which is not exported by expo-asset@latest in this env. Because of this, NO runtime Playwright/UI exercise of the 12 tabs was possible this iteration — every page navigation produces a blank DOM (root div never hydrates) and document.body.innerText is empty. The Android prod bundle is still healthy: GET /index.bundle?platform=android&dev=false&minify=true → HTTP 200, 4,267,623 bytes (matches the 4.3MB the main agent reported). iOS bundle also compiles (1355 modules per expo.out.log). Main agent's note flagged the expo-asset quirk as 'OK to ignore' but in practice it makes the web preview untestable, so I switched to deep static review for the 12 tabs + supporting infra. STATIC REVIEW VERDICT: structural refactor is clean. All 11 visible-tab files (nexushome, scripts, butler, terminal, knowledge, settings, fileshare, logs, skins, support, builder) export ErrorBoundary from @/components/ui/TabErrorBoundary so a render crash will not white-screen the app. All 10 files listed by the refactor correctly use 'expo-file-system/legacy' (fileshare, scripts, nexushome, settings, DownloadButtons, QuickSendCard, nexusCrawlerEngine, kbGrowthTracker, scriptPersistence, userScripts). No deprecated expo-av / expo-barcode-scanner / expo-background-fetch imports anywhere in app+components+services+utils. KnowledgeDomain type is correctly inlined at services/knowledgeAccumulator.ts:9–13 (replacing the removed butlerKnowledge module) and used at lines 457+471. ButlerWelcomeHub is imported by butler.tsx and rendered when messages.length===0 (butler.tsx:1217–1224). QuickButlerBar exposes testIDs quickbar-input/quickbar-send; FuturisticTabBar emits testID=tab-{routeName}; ConnectionBadge is mounted globally in (tabs)/_layout.tsx with pointerEvents='box-none' wrapper. expo-file-system 19.0.23 has the /legacy submodule present (node_modules/expo-file-system/src/legacy + build/legacy exist) so the legacy imports resolve. Hidden routes (terminal, fileshare, support) declared with href:null in (tabs)/_layout.tsx are reachable by direct URL as expected. ROOT pages all present: /main-menu, /welcome, /tutorial, /privacy-policy, /terms, /data-safety, /onboarding_v2. ONE concrete bundling-grade bug found: components/ui/NexusTips.tsx declares default export twice (line 257 `export default function HUDTips(...)` AND line 277 `export { HUDTips as default }`). Metro currently tolerates it but ESLint flags it 'Multiple default exports' [import/export]; it is consumed by terminal.tsx via components/index.ts. TWO minor lint-only issues (not runtime): fileshare.tsx:722 has a dead function `_ScheduleSection_removed` that still calls useState/useCallback/useEffect at the top (5+ rule-of-hooks errors), and terminal.tsx:563/598 have JSX comments outside braces. Neither is reachable at runtime. Deprecated packages detected: NONE. SafeAreaView issues detected: NONE (tabs/_layout uses useSafeAreaInsets correctly).

ui_bugs.issue: Duplicate default export: line 257 declares `export default function HUDTips` and line 277 re-declares `export { HUDTips as default }`. Metro currently bundles, but Babel/SWC strict mode or a future Metro upgrade will reject this. Reference: /app/frontend/components/ui/NexusTips.tsx:257 and :277. Used by terminal tab via components/index.ts.

integration_issues.issue: GET /index.bundle?platform=web returns HTTP 500 — Metro error 'Missing "./build/resolveAssetSource" specifier in "expo-asset" package'. Triggered by expo-image (imported by components/RobotThemeUI.tsx ← app/onboarding_v2.tsx). Result: NO tab can be exercised via the preview URL — root div never hydrates, body innerText is empty. Android bundle is unaffected (HTTP 200, 4.27MB). This made it impossible to surface runtime logger.dump() / @butler_auto_error_logs_v1 entries this iteration.

action_items: (P0 BLOCKER for any future web-preview testing) Either swap `import { Image as ExpoImage } from 'expo-image'` in /app/frontend/components/RobotThemeUI.tsx for react-native's Image (web safe) on web platform, OR add a metro resolver alias mapping 'expo-asset/./build/resolveAssetSource' → 'expo-asset' index, OR upgrade/downgrade expo-asset so its package.json `exports` map includes './build/resolveAssetSource'. Without this, no testing agent can validate the 12-tab UI via the preview URL — only Android EAS builds will be verifiable. (Main agent already flagged this as 'OK to ignore' but please reconsider — it eliminates the only QA surface available in preview.)

Fix duplicate default export in /app/frontend/components/ui/NexusTips.tsx — remove either line 257's `export default function HUDTips(...)` keyword or the redundant line 277 `export { HUDTips as default }`. Keep only one default export.

Delete (or actually wire up) the dead `_ScheduleSection_removed` function in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/fileshare.tsx:722–810. It calls useState/useCallback/useEffect at top-level outside any rendered component and is never imported — ESLint flags 7 rule-of-hooks errors and it bloats the file by ~90 lines.
Wrap or remove the bare JSX comments at /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/terminal.tsx:563 and :598 (move into {/* */} braces).

(Carried from iter 2/3, still applicable) 9 of 11 tab files exceed the 700-line guideline — settings.tsx 4078, scripts.tsx 3645, knowledge.tsx 3403, nexushome.tsx 1844, logs.tsx 1585, support.tsx 1511, builder.tsx 1383, fileshare.tsx 1389, butler.tsx 1278. Strongly recommend extracting per-section subcomponents under /components/{settings,scripts,knowledge,...}/*.
critical_code_review_comments: expo-file-system/legacy migration looks complete and consistent. All 10 callers use `import * as FileSystem from 'expo-file-system/legacy'` and the API surface they use (readAsStringAsync, writeAsStringAsync, EncodingType.UTF8/Base64, cacheDirectory) is present in the legacy submodule of expo-file-system@19.0.23 — no usage of removed root-namespace symbols was found.
No remaining imports of `expo-av`, `expo-barcode-scanner`, or `expo-background-fetch` were found anywhere under /app/frontend/app, components, services, utils — deprecated-package surface is clean.

(tabs)/_layout.tsx correctly registers 8 visible tabs (nexushome/scripts/butler/knowledge/logs/builder/skins/settings) and 4 hidden routes (terminal/index/fileshare/support) with href:null — matches the 12-tab spec in the request.

Every visible tab file exports `ErrorBoundary` from @/components/ui/TabErrorBoundary, and TabErrorBoundary.tsx implements BOTH a class component (componentDidCatch routes to autoErrorLogger) AND the Expo-Router `{ error, retry }` signature — render crashes will now surface as the 'TAB CRASHED' recovery card instead of a white screen. ✓

knowledgeAccumulator.ts:9–13 correctly inlines `type KnowledgeDomain = { name: string; findings: any[]; [key: string]: any }` after the removal of ./butlerKnowledge — TypeScript references at lines 457 and 471 type-check against this shape.

butler.tsx:1217–1224 correctly renders <ButlerWelcomeHub /> when `messages.length === 0`, passing accentColor, isConnected, modelName=null, onSendPrompt and onBuildScript — empty-state path is intact.

QuickButlerBar.tsx exposes BUTLER_PREFILL_KEY ('@butler_prefill_prompt') + testIDs quickbar-input/quickbar-send; FuturisticTabBar.tsx emits testID=`tab-${route.name}` on each pill — testing surface is intact (could not be exercised this run due to web-bundle 500).

utils/logger.ts ring buffer + logger.dump() and services/autoErrorLogger.ts (persists to @butler_auto_error_logs_v1) are both present and correctly wired into TabErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch — when web preview works again, these will be the right hooks to read for residual runtime errors.
(tabs)/_layout.tsx correctly wraps ConnectionBadge in a pointerEvents='box-none' floating container so it does not eat taps on underlying tab content. ✓

success_rate.frontend: Live UI tests executed: 0/12 (web bundle 500). Android bundle compile: PASS (HTTP 200, 4.27MB). iOS bundle compile: PASS (per metro log). Static review: 11/11 tab files structurally healthy (TabErrorBoundary wired). expo-file-system/legacy migration: 10/10 files PASS. Deprecated-pkg sweep: 0 hits (PASS). KnowledgeDomain inline type: PASS. ButlerWelcomeHub empty-state wiring: PASS. testID surface on QuickButlerBar/FuturisticTabBar: PASS. Bundling-grade bugs found: 1 (NexusTips duplicate default export).

seed_data_creation: Attempted to seed @butler_onboarding_done_v2, @butler_consent_v2, @butler_terms_accepted_v1, @butler_privacy_accepted_v1, @butler_age_confirmed_v1, @butler_lan_consent_v1, @butler_server_privacy_accepted_v1 = '1' in localStorage and reload — successfully written, but UI never rendered because /index.bundle returned HTTP 500.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Web preview is currently UNUSABLE because /index.bundle?platform=web returns HTTP 500 with 'Missing "./build/resolveAssetSource" specifier in "expo-asset" package'. Trace: expo-image → react-native/Libraries/Image/resolveAssetSource → expo-asset/./build/resolveAssetSource (not in package.json exports). Triggered by components/RobotThemeUI.tsx (line 14) ← app/onboarding_v2.tsx (line ~import @/components/RobotThemeUI). Until main agent removes/replaces the expo-image import in RobotThemeUI OR adds a Metro resolver alias, no Playwright UI exercise is possible. Android bundle works fine — use `curl -o- 'https://startup-fixer.preview.emergentagent.com/index.bundle?platform=android&dev=false&minify=true'` to verify (returns 4.27MB, HTTP 200). Once the web bundle is fixed, the testIDs to drive are: tab-{nexushome|scripts|butler|knowledge|logs|builder|skins|settings}, quickbar-input, quickbar-send, hero-scan-qr, quick-access-{scripts|butler|knowledge|fileshare}, qsend-{clipboard|text|file}. Seed the 7 onboarding flags in localStorage and reload to land on (tabs). Butler tab has a one-time 'Local AI Behavioral Notice' modal — tap 'ENTER BUTLER AI' to dismiss. /app/frontend/components/ui/NexusTips.tsx still has the duplicate-default-export bug if not yet fixed by main agent.
rca_of_issue: Web bundle 500 RCA — expo-image imports `react-native/Libraries/Image/resolveAssetSource` (an internal RN module not supported on web), which Metro tries to redirect to `expo-asset/./build/resolveAssetSource`. Inspecting /app/frontend/node_modules/expo-asset/package.json shows its `exports` map only declares '.', './app.plugin', './tools/hashAssetFiles', and './package.json' — no './build/resolveAssetSource' entry exists. resolve.exports therefore throws 'Missing "./build/resolveAssetSource" specifier' and Metro emits HTTP 500. Reproduction: `curl -i 'https://startup-fixer.preview.emergentagent.com/index.bundle?platform=web&dev=true&hot=false&lazy=true&transform.engine=hermes&transform.routerRoot=app&unstable_transformProfile=hermes-stable'` → 500 + InternalError JSON. Mitigation options (pick one): (a) replace `import { Image as ExpoImage } from 'expo-image'` in RobotThemeUI.tsx with React Native's own Image when Platform.OS==='web' (gated import) — preserves expo-image on native; (b) add a `resolver.alias` or `resolver.extraNodeModules` entry in metro.config.js mapping the missing specifier to expo-asset's index (but user said do not modify metro.config); (c) upgrade expo-asset to a version whose package.json exports map includes './build/resolveAssetSource'. NexusTips RCA — line 257 declares the default via function-decl form and line 277 re-declares it via named-as-default form; ES module spec allows only one default export per module. Likely artifact of a search-and-replace that left both forms. Fix: delete line 277.
